---
title: "Zero declination, please"
author:
  - name: Ralph Straumann
    url: https://ralphstraumann.ch
date: "2026-03-18 21:12"
image: "rotated-map-sheets.png"
description: "Fun explorations: David O'Sullivan gets nerd-sniped by an #xkcd comic into creating a #map #projection where magnetic north and true north are perfectly aligned everywhere."
---


Something ~~lighter~~ less serious, but still instructive: Staying within the 
xkcd frame of reference, I would say David O’Sullivan was successfully
"nerd-sniped"[^nerdsniped] by [xkcd 3207](https://xkcd.com/3207/) 
([explanation][explanation]):

![xkcd 3207: "Bad Map Projection: Zero Declination"](xkcd-3207.png 'xkcd 3207: "Bad Map Projection: Zero Declination"')

Using R, David [tries to create the zero declination map][article] proposed in 
the xkcd. That is, a map featuring a projection so that at each location on the 
Earth surface has a [declination][declination] of 0°. In yet other words: ... 
so that the [magnetic north][magneticnorth] and [true north][truenorth] are 
exactly aligned everywhere. 

What follows is a (fun!) deep dive into different approaches to the problem. I 
enjoyed the somewhat whimsical premise, the joy of experimentation, the results 
(complete with code, for those interested!), and the lessons David draws from 
the exercise. I won’t spoil the fun by giving those away, but I will say that 
[David's article][article] is a nice example of how to approach a problem 
through experimental iteration, and how to learn from the process.

[^nerdsniped]: See [xkcd 356](https://xkcd.com/356/) and, if needed, its 
[explanation](https://explainxkcd.com/356/). Looking at David's [postscript](https://geospatialstuff.com/posts/2026/03/18/#postscript) this seems to happen from time to time. Tip: xkcd 
explanations can always be found by adding `explain` at the beginning of the 
URL, i.e. `xkcd.com/356` &rarr; `explainxkcd.com/356`.

[article]: https://geospatialstuff.com/posts/2026/03/18/

[explanation]: https://explainxkcd.com/3207/

[declination]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Magnetic_declination

[magneticnorth]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/North_magnetic_pole

[truenorth]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/True_north
